frameworks.name: multiple-systems theory of category learning
frameworks.one_line: qualitatively distinct neural and cognitive systems mediate different category-learning tasks

frameworks.name: explicit rule-based reasoning
frameworks.one_line: frontal-striatal circuits using working memory and executive attention

frameworks.name: procedural learning
frameworks.one_line: feedback-driven implicit learning for information-integration categories

frameworks.name: perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: visual cortical plasticity engaged by prototype distortion tasks

core_question: Do different category-learning tasks recruit qualitatively distinct cognitive and neural systems?
key_assumptions: category structure determines which learning system is engaged
cognitive, neuropsychological, and neuroimaging evidence can dissociate systems
manipulations: type of category-learning task (rule-based vs information-integration vs prototype distortion vs weather prediction)
key_findings: rule-based tasks recruit frontal-striatal circuits, working memory, executive attention
information-integration tasks rely on procedural learning sensitive to feedback nature and timing
prototype distortion tasks induce visual cortical perceptual learning
multiple strategies can succeed on the weather prediction task
interpretations: human category learning is mediated by multiple qualitatively distinct systems
